summary: Strong early‑80s performance with standout Shakedown Street, Cassidy and Fire, captured on a good‑ish audience tape that’s punchy but shows some EQ/hiss artifacts.
review: The show is widely praised as one of the best pre‑coma Brent era concerts, featuring hot renditions of Shakedown Street, Cassidy and Fire on the Mountain, plus rare appearances of China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider and a Good Lovin' encore. The Charlie Miller‑processed audience tape delivers extra presence and deep bass, though a few listeners note excessive EQ, hiss and possible Dolby decoding issues.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers rate the performance highly (5‑star) and cite strong setlist highlights, while recording quality receives mixed feedback; overall the show’s excellence outweighs the technical flaws.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Described as having more presence, punch and deep bass than other sources; however some note heavy EQ, hissy/smeared sound and possible Dolby decoding problems.

standout_songs: China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider
show_quality.setlist_flow: Early‑80s jam vibe with a solid first set and a second set highlighted by the rare CC > Rider sequence and an uncommon Good Lovin' encore, delivering a strong overall flow.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Mentioned as 'in Dah House', indicating an energetic guitar performance.
band_member_comments.Bob: His slide work on China Cat Sunflower was described as a bit hard to take.
